BRAND & DESIGN SYSTEM · v1 · FROM ENSO LABS

Strategy → Ship

The media publishing brand of Enso Labs — where strategy becomes production. Media × advertising × AI. Modern, but human.

This is the living visual guideline. It supersedes the legacy brand. Canonical lock: docs/brand/STRATEGY-TO-SHIP-BRAND-LOCK.md · tokens: tokens.css

Confidentiality (locked): the client Gore must always render as "a Fortune 500 manufacturer" in any public-facing asset, copy, or article — never the client name.

01 — Logotype
StrategyShip
primary wordmark — Space Mono 700, coral swept ribbon replaces the word "to"
app icon — coral tile + white ribbon (not a letter monogram)

Lowercase handle: strategy▸ship (Space Mono; the ▸ is the small-format ribbon fallback). Endorsement lockup pairs the wordmark with a hairline and FROM ENSO LABS. Always Space Mono 700; never Lora; never an arrow glyph or letter-pair monogram; never recolor the ribbon; never lock up with Enso's chevron.

02 — Color

Warm paper-and-ink editorial palette with one decisive signal. Coral is a signal (~5–10% of a composition), never a fill. Teal is the parent-brand link only.

Paper
#F7F1E6
reading ground
Paper 2
#EEE5D3
cards / fills
Ink
#1E1813
text / dark ground
Ink Deep
#16110B
OG / social
Ship Coral
#F0512E
THE signal
Ledger Amber
#E0A23C
2nd accent
Slate
#79705F
metadata
Line
#DDD2BC
hairlines
Enso Teal
#5CE0D2
parent link only
03 — Typography
Display — Space Mono 700 (a mono voice on a clean frame)
The headline is the art.
Body / UI — Inter Tight (shared with Enso)
Clear, modern, humanist. Carries the article body, captions, and interface — the connective tissue between the serif headline and the machine metadata.
Metadata / signal — JetBrains Mono (the machine voice)
KICKER · DATELINE · v5 · CONFIDENCE 0.92 · SOURCE
04 — Voice

A smart colleague who ships — not a vendor who pitches.

  • Plain-English headlines. No jargon ("frontier model" → "AI model"), no hype (revolutionary, game-changing, transformative).
  • Studio "we", first person. Sourced claims, inline links, a buyer-translation ("what this means for a CTO").
  • Name the human-in-the-loop. Close with Powered by Enso Labs.
05 — Devices
SHIPPED · v5
the stamp — inked, coral, rotated −4°
strategyship
the swept ribbon — strategy to ship (replaces the word "to"; not an arrow glyph)
v5 LIVE DRAFT
version tags
06 — Imagery

Warm-graded documentary photography — real people, real rooms. Type sits over the image. Never stock "AI", glowing brains, or robots.

FIELD NOTES
A founder demos a Codex 5.5 workflow.
07 — OG / social cards (1200×630)
dark "ship card" — feeds, default
light editorial — articles, calmer

09 — Publishing standards (per platform)

LinkedIn — the lead surface

CAROUSEL/DOC 1080×1350 · NATIVE, NEVER A LINK POST · ~6.6% ENG (HIGHEST)
  • Lead with the carousel cover (Space Mono headline + coral stamp); one atomic idea per slide; close on a CTA slide.
  • Pre-warm the OG via LinkedIn Post Inspector before posting any article link.
  • M/W/F cadence; hook in the first line, no outbound link in body (drop it in comments).

X / Twitter

CARD 500×280 (OG) · THREAD-NATIVE
  • Open with the plain-English claim; one chart or stamp image per thread; link last.

Substack & Medium

HEADER 1200×400 · CANONICAL = ensolabs.ai/insights
  • Cross-post the full article but set rel=canonical back to ensolabs.ai/insights to protect SEO.
  • Header uses the light editorial register; footer keeps "Powered by Enso Labs."

Enso Labs home page

SHOWCASE MODULE — "LATEST FROM STRATEGY TO SHIP"
  • Feature the newest 2–3 insights with the dark OG cards — the homepage proves the studio ships and publishes.
  • Endorsement, not takeover: Strategy to Ship is a labeled section within the Enso (navy/teal) home, linking to /insights.
STRATEGY → SHIP — a publishing engine from Enso LabsPowered by Enso Labs · ensolabs.ai/insights
